title: Copying automations to other boards
excerpt:
Hello everyone, 
is there any way that I can copy an automation to another board, so I would not need to create the same automation for all boards? 
Thank you!

posts.author: VeronikaKunova (Veronika Kunova)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello everyone,

is there any way that I can copy an automation to another board, so I would not need to create the same automation for all boards?

Thank you!

posts.author: Marius.Manic (Marius)
posts.cookedHtml: You could make it a template and then tuning it for the new boards.

posts.author: VeronikaKunova (Veronika Kunova)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes, but if i make some changes in the template then it doesn’t reflect in the boards created by the template without new changes, is that right?

Thank you for your help!

posts.author: Marius.Manic (Marius)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes, the template is merely a starting point for new instances.
In case of major changes, maybe it is esier to set up a new (modified) template and to use it in all boards (deleting the old ones, of course).

posts.author: Colleen (Colleen Geyer)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes! I would also love to see this! Even as a template, I can’t get it on to another board. I’d love the option to duplicate and move to another board!

posts.author: paperdandelion (Claire LeMaster)
posts.cookedHtml:
So this is actually possible but not if your second board already exists. When you fully duplicate a board, it also duplicates the details of the automation you’ve set up.

This may not help you if your boards all have very different parameters, but if you’re setting up several similar boards then it might be worth turning the whole board into the template, which will capture all the automation details as well. In some situations that might be easier than copying over all the details of the old automation.

You would then be able to transfer any items or groups from other boards, as long as your columns are the same.

posts.author: jholley (jessica.holley@ellucian.com)
posts.cookedHtml: Does anyone know if this has been updated as a feature yet? I’m trying to do this, but can’t.

posts.author: wenzday (wenzday)
posts.cookedHtml: There are plenty of reasons why this should be a feature. Instances where the automation is specific and making it a template wouldn’t make sense.

posts.author: wenzday (wenzday)
posts.cookedHtml: I would also like to see this as a feature, to copy the automation to another board.

posts.author: D.Red (devonred@umich.edu)
posts.cookedHtml: This is frustrating. I also would like this useful feature…

posts.author: matty.tilbrook (Matty Tilbrook)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have 158 automations on my current board and need to create a new version for next year. I saved it as a template and used that to create a new board (which seems to be the only option), but only about 50 automations carried over.

Chat support suggested recreating them manually—but they took a long time to set up, and doing it all again will take ages.

Why isn’t there a simple way to duplicate a board with all automations intact?

posts.author: matty.tilbrook (Matty Tilbrook)
posts.cookedHtml: Seriously, this is taking forever

posts.author: matty.tilbrook (Matty Tilbrook)
posts.cookedHtml: You can’t save all the automations as templates, and even when you do, you still have to reconfigure them — so it doesn’t save much time compared to building them from scratch. Why not just let us copy and paste them to a new board?

posts.author: MonicaP (Monica Pepe)
posts.cookedHtml:
So I tried duplicating a board with automations. They did not carry over to my duplicated board.

Then I tried making my board a template, yay the automations were saved to the template.

Then I tried using the template and NONE of the automations came forward into my new board.

Is it me?
